prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, commonly used for stock price forecasting. We assume an annualized drift (expected return) of 10% and an annualized volatility of 25%, which are typical for a large-cap stock like DE. The point estimate is calculated as the expected value under this drift over 21 days. The confidence intervals are derived from the log-normal distribution property of stock prices in the GBM model, using the specified daily drift and volatility (adjusted for the mean of the log-normal distribution) and corresponding Z-scores for each confidence level. These parameters reflect general market expectations and historical behavior, but actual future prices may deviate significantly due to unforeseen market events or company-specific news.
